title: Kth Smallest Element in a Sorted Matrix
hints: Notice that for any value x, you can count elements less than or equal to x in O(n) time using the sorted property of rows and columns.
Use binary search on the value range from matrix[0][0] to matrix[n-1][n-1] to find the smallest value where the count of smaller or equal elements is at least k.
The count function starts at the bottom-left corner; move up if the current element is less than or equal to the target, otherwise move right.
